title: glaucium elegans subsp. elegans
intro: Glaucium elegans subsp. elegans, also known as the Greek poppy or horn poppy, is a perennial plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, containing alkaloids such as thebaine and codeine, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Glaucium elegans subsp. elegans can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues.
